mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, shorter discount rates on future earnings decline and equity valuations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also easing, because disinflation reduces the chance of renewed tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if declining FEDFUNDS does not coincide with positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the return edge disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure equities downward over the next month. The effect is a demand shock channel rather than a valuation channel and should be visible even if prices are stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE spikes of at least 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and inventory rebuilding, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 prices over a 60-day horizon. The signal is strongest when PAYEMS is also growing, confirming broad-based production and labor momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upswings in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce higher-than-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS signals stronger retail demand and revenue growth, which should translate into positive equity returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if sales growth is driven by price inflation rather than real demand, or if labor weakening offsets consumption strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average by at least 1%
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales breakouts are not followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the edge vanishes when UNRATE is rising at the same time.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates labor-market tightness without immediate recession risk, supporting household spending and earnings over the next week. This is a short-horizon momentum signal that should fail when payroll gains are accompanied by a simultaneous rise in joblessness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or falling while PAYEMS rises over the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-unemployment/high-payroll condition does not yield positive 5d SPX returns above baseline, or if the signal is neutral when UNRATE and PAYEMS move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated relative to recent history, discount rates and financial conditions tighten, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days. The effect should be clearest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because then policy restraint is the dominant macro force.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 4.5% is not associated with negative 60d SPX returns, or if returns remain positive even when CPIAUCSL is flat and rates are restrictive.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with weak RSAFS signals a broad demand slowdown, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month as revenue and earnings expectations are revised down. The mechanism is a demand-led recession warning and should be strongest when both series soften together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below trend and RSAFS is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 20d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect fully and the interaction is unnecessary.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior advance signals a cooling housing cycle, which should weaken cyclically sensitive growth expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal is most informative when industrial production is also soft, confirming economy-wide deceleration.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% from its 3-month high
failure_condition: Reject if housing rollovers of more than 5% from a 3-month high are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when INDPRO is simultaneously weakening.
